spec_match.rationale: True A/B: deprecated `inset-area` on the left panel, new `position-area` on the right, shared direction value. Demonstrates the rename's behaviour-preserving promise.

interactive.rationale: Direction <select> drives both panels; real comparison.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises 'A/B with same anchor, popover, direction values' — page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: The hardcoded .pop-new uses `position-area: top` but the JS only updates inline style; need to verify the JS actually flips position-area too. Looking at the snippet, .pop-new has a hardcoded `position-area: top` declaration which would be overridden by the inline-style update.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables; .tag-dep / .tag-new both set background and color together.

openQuestions.title: Verify both panels actually update on direction change
openQuestions.detail: The CSS has `.pop-new { position-area: top; }` hardcoded. If the JS only writes inline-style updates, that's fine — but if it doesn't update .pop-new, the right panel will stay at 'top' while the left panel moves. Spot-check that the JS sets both panels' position-area / inset-area properly.

summary: Resolved the required ChromeStatus reference; 1 follow-up question(s) remain.
